title: Making book cloth from fabric
sources: Douglas Cockerell, Bookbinding and the Care of Books (1901) — lining and preparing cover materials
notes: The classic laminate: fabric + a thin tissue backing to stop adhesive strike-through to the face. Wheat-paste or diluted PVA applied to the tissue, fabric laid face-down on a clean board, tissue smoothed onto the back. Dries under a board. Result is sturdy book cloth you can use on a case binding.
